item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it but i think it needs to state that somnabulism is sleepwalking

item.title: Definitely getting in my favourites list
item.content:encoded:
Funny, psycho-terror, and that review though
Hahahahahahaah +1

I'm a sucker for scips involving the mind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Soo…It's Uber…for ghosts. Not gonna lie, that's pretty awesome. Heck, I think I might sign up for that if I could stipulate that no harm would come to my body and it would avoid illegal activity or interaction with people I know.

Also, I'm pretty sure that there's an SCP that's some sort of fourth state of REM sleep where the subject's dreams become reality. (And the Foundation is worried enough about it that lucid dreamers with the ability are terminated with extreme prejudice) It might be cool to toss something in about the danger of the two interacting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're good! Don't worry about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So basically, when you use it, you enter into a dream state, but at the same time, you allow something to be hosted in your body.

That's creepy, and I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Please, ignore me. I wanted to make a comment on the SCP, but didn't knew that "reply" was to reply to another comment rather than the page.

Sorry! I'm new

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
All other subjects who have used SCP-2876 will wake up in possession of a business card reading "Thanks for being a great host! We'll be back real soon!"

I feel like this could have been the core of a much stronger article. I also have to confess I was expecting this to go all Cabinet of Dr. Caligari up until the end. As this was, it focused on the least interesting aspects of how a creation like this might function and relate to Onereoi.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Done. Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Of course, I made this for your article, wrong date included. (This is very recognizably google play but none of the "obscure" app stores have good looking reviews)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oooh, can I use that?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, after you send the post, it looks completely differently. I made this in Google Play:

http://i.imgur.com/fHru5kD.png

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yea, it kinda looks fake, but for me it doesn't matter, so do what you want to the image.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It actually came from me going to leave a review on the Apple app store, taking a screenshot of the text box and using image editing software to remove the cursor. If other people think the fake review looks too fake I can try to make it more convincing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm sorry, but the review on the app store looks very fake. Like someone transparently made this in paint.

Can you "inspect element" and rewrite the text on any review in any of the "lesser-known" app stores (ie not Apple, MS or Google)?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nice to see the non-corporeals getting out and about. +1

Something wrong here though:

Any references to a parallel app was indicated through patch notes included with the revision were taken out in subsequent updates.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks to Anaxagoras for helping me smooth this one out a little more, fixing some awkward phrasing and generally making it leaner and meaner. Thanks again, man!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think the addendum really makes it interesting and steers it away from "COOL thing what makes you do BAD thing if you use it too much," which it was leaning towards without it.

also the idea of paying for dreams with cognitive ability is kinda eerie…I'm a fan.

+1, very well done

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nevermind, I just can't read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What are you referring to? The paragraph after the table talks about what happens to dead subjects, then moves on to describing what happens to injured and unharmed ones.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ahh, I knew you'd figure something out. :) I think this is the first Oneiroi skip I actually like! (And that review, my god.)

My one question: How can the subjects die in ways that leave their host body not dead?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ahahahaha.

I don't know what aspect of this I like more, people buying custom dreams from Oneiroi like you would rent a movie or video game, or Oneiroi renting out their head space subscribers like some kind of dream time share.

Either way, this is an enjoyable article. Enthusiastic +1.

(Also, top-notch on the image work Cyan)

item.title: Re: image creation post
item.content:encoded:
A+ job on that. I gasped out loud when I saw it.

ur so talented wew

item.title: image creation post
item.content:encoded: Logo is pulled from this article. Skull is from Wikipedia and licensed for reuse. Screenshot is from my cell phone. All was edited in Adobe Photoshop CC 2017 and is CC-compliant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Was nice to be involved in the process here, help it evolve. Turning it into an app was a clever move, and allowed you to do some really nice stuff. That splash screen is killer.

+1, obv

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to Cyantreuse for the sick image and Rimple, AbsentmindedNihilist, TL333s, Conwell, DrBleep and Randomini for looking this over. This is actually the first Oneiroi SCP I've written since they became a GoI so I hope I can still manage the group I came up with. Thanks for reading!
Also credit to Tuomey for the title

e: Thank you thefran for the second image

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted at "General fixes and dithering about."
